w:r.w:t: Welcome to our home. We hope you have a comfortable, relaxing and memorable stay in Bristol.
Key collection and entry details are shared separately before arrival.

wp:docPr.name: Picture 1
wp:docPr.descr:
A screenshot of a qr code
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 1
pic:cNvPr.descr:
A screenshot of a qr code
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

w:r.w:t: Parking outside the house is residents only. If you are bringing a car, please send the registration number in advance so a visitor permit can be arranged.
The heating is on a timed programme and linked to a thermostat in the kitchen. It can be overridden by raising the target temperature.
Please message us if anything is unclear or if something in the house needs attention.

A taxi is the easiest option. Uber picks up from the short-stay car park. Arrow Cars are outside the terminal. BDriven can be pre-booked and follows flight arrivals. The Airport Flyer bus is the lowest-cost public transport option.
The house is walkable through Queen Square and the harbour, though a taxi or Uber is easier with bags.
Please let us know before arrival if you will need visitor parking.
• Please feel free to use the kitchen appliances and utensils you need.
• Tea, instant coffee and sugar are on the shelf above the kettle. Coffee pods are in the wooden container next to the Nespresso machine.
• Milk and a few basic essentials may be in the fridge. If you finish something, please replace it.
• Dishwasher tablets and cleaning products are under the sink.
• The washing machine and tumble dryer are in the little outbuilding to your left as you walk out into the garden.
• The tumble dryer is a condenser dryer. Please empty the water container after each run. Pull out the drawer in the top left corner and empty it into the outside drain.
• Mop and bucket are in the utility room.
• Iron: kitchen cabinet to the right of the sink. Ironing board: behind the fridge.
• The small bin by the front door has two compartments: the right side is for general waste, and the left side is for mixed recycling such as paper, cardboard, tins and plastic containers.
• Food waste goes in the small food caddy.
• For short stays, there is no need to take rubbish out. For longer stays, the larger bins are in the black shed on the left side of the garden steps.
• Collection day is Thursday. If you do put bins out, Wednesday night is the right time.
• Most of the lamps in the living room are connected to one switch and controlled by a remote left out somewhere visible
. Main overhead light switch is behind the plant next the stereo system.
• Board games are behind the sofa.
• Use the TV remote to switch on the television. The Sky box has its own remote.
• Alexa speakers are in the kitchen, living room and bedrooms. Saying “Alexa, play…” should start music through Spotify.
• In the top bedroom, the Velux blinds close using a remote that should be left on top of the mirror above the sinks.
• A special pole is used to open the Velux windows for fresh air.
• In good weather, please enjoy the garden.
• Cushions for the seats, coal for the BBQ and the hanging hammock seat are stored in the shed at the far end of the garden.
• The switch for the hanging garden lights is on the wall between the utility room and the bin shed.
What guests usually need to know
Pods are in the wooden container next to the machine.
In the outbuilding off the garden.
Please empty the water drawer after each cycle.
TV remote for the television, separate remote for Sky.
Use the remote left on the mirror shelf.
Manuals: Exact model numbers are not listed in the original guide, so model-specific manuals can be added later.
A few good places within easy walking distance of the house:
• Bristol Lido - good for a swim, breakfast, lunch, dinner or just soaking up the atmosphere.
• From the house, head uphill towards Clifton village via Polygon Lane and Hensman Hill.
• Browse the independent shops, cafes and charity shops around the village.
• Continue to the Clifton Suspension Bridge, one of Bristol’s most iconic landmarks.
• If you have the energy, continue towards Bristol Museum, Wills Memorial Building, Park Street, Cabot Tower, College Green and the cathedral.
• Walk downhill from the house towards the harbour and make a loop around the waterfront.
• Possible stops include SS Great Britain, M Shed, Wapping Wharf, the Matthew, Arnolfini, Millennium Square, Bristol Cathedral and St Nicholas Market.
• This route is also great for street art spotting and people-watching.
• The Bristol Ferry is an optional extra if you would like part of the route by water.
We The Curious has reopened, so Millennium Square is once again a good stop on a harbour walk.
Bristol is famous for its street art, including Banksy works and many others. These self-guided resources are a good starting point:
Where Goes Rose: street art guide
• Please leave used towels together in the bathroom or utility area.
• Switch off lights and small appliances where possible.
• Lock doors and windows before departure.
• If anything needs attention, please send us a quick message before you leave.
